title: Minimalist Spines Clean Up via ax.spines
naive_code:
# Default heavy box borders around the chart
fig, ax = plt.subplots()
ax.plot(x, y)
wizard_code:
# Minimalist Top & Right Spines Removal
fig, ax = plt.subplots()
ax.plot(x, y)
ax.spines[['top', 'right']].set_visible(False)
hinglish_explanation: Chart ke side borders ('spines') data ink ratio ruin karte hain. `ax.spines[['top', 'right']].set_visible(False)` se chart modern aur minimalist lagta hai!
ai_prompt: Show how to remove all four spines and ticks from a Matplotlib axis for clean styling.

title: Prevent Axis Labels Cutoff with bbox_inches='tight'
naive_code:
# Default export often cuts off long labels or legends
plt.savefig('plot.png')
wizard_code:
# Auto-Adjusted Bounding Box Export
plt.savefig('plot.png', bbox_inches='tight', dpi=300)
hinglish_explanation: `savefig()` ke default export settings titles aur legends ko cut kar dete hain. `bbox_inches='tight'` figure margins ko boundaries ke size par adjust karta hai!
ai_prompt: Explain savefig parameters like dpi, transparent=True, and bbox_inches='tight' in Matplotlib.

title: Complex Layout Grids with plt.subplot_mosaic()
naive_code:
# Messy grid sizing using subplot2grid or nested specs
ax1 = plt.subplot2grid((2, 2), (0, 0), colspan=2)
ax2 = plt.subplot2grid((2, 2), (1, 0))
wizard_code:
# Clean Text-Based Layout Definition (Python 3.9+)
fig, axd = plt.subplot_mosaic([['header', 'header'], ['left', 'right']])
# Access axes: axd['header'].plot(...)
hinglish_explanation: Complex subplots grid layout design karne ke liye ugly integers coordinate logic ki jagah direct text string schema layout use karo!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate plt.subplot_mosaic() with empty placeholders and spanning configurations.

title: Double Y-Axis Overlay with ax.twinx()
naive_code:
# Drawing both series on same scale (squashes lower range values)
ax.plot(x, temperature)
ax.plot(x, sales) # squashed!
wizard_code:
# Dual Axis Overlay Chart
fig, ax1 = plt.subplots()
ax1.plot(x, temperature, color='red')
ax2 = ax1.twinx()
ax2.plot(x, sales, color='blue')
hinglish_explanation: Do variables jinki scales bilkul alag hon (e.g. Temperature in Celsius & Sales in Dollars), unhe `ax1.twinx()` se shared X-axis par scale overlays de sakte hain!
ai_prompt: Show how to create a twinx() dual Y-axis plot with custom colors for both axes.

title: Visualize Confidence Intervals with ax.fill_between()
naive_code:
# Plotting upper and lower error bounds as simple dotted lines
ax.plot(x, mean)
ax.plot(x, upper, '--')
ax.plot(x, lower, '--')
wizard_code:
# Highlighted Shaded Error Margin Area
ax.plot(x, mean, color='blue')
ax.fill_between(x, lower, upper, color='blue', alpha=0.15)
hinglish_explanation: Time-series forecasts ya standard deviations error margins highlight karne ke liye bounds lines ki jagah translucent background shade `fill_between` apply karo!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate fill_between() with conditional arguments using where parameter.

title: Prevent PDF Export Bloat with rasterized=True
naive_code:
# Vector PDF files with millions of scatter points load incredibly slow
ax.scatter(x, y)
plt.savefig('report.pdf')
wizard_code:
# Hybrid Vector-Raster PDF Serialization
ax.scatter(x, y, rasterized=True)
plt.savefig('report.pdf', dpi=300)
hinglish_explanation: Vector PDFs me 1 lakh scatter points individual math objects hote hain jo viewer crash karte hain. `rasterized=True` scatter coordinate grid pixels compile kar deta hai!
ai_prompt: Explain vector vs raster rendering in Matplotlib and when to use rasterized=True.

title: Zoom In Details with Inset Axes via ax.inset_axes()
naive_code:
# Rendering a separate full figure for detailing specific regions
# ... manual second plot setup
wizard_code:
# Embedded Zoom-In Inset Box
axins = ax.inset_axes([0.6, 0.6, 0.35, 0.35])
axins.plot(x, y)
axins.set_xlim(2, 3); axins.set_ylim(4, 5)
ax.indicate_inset_zoom(axins, edgecolor="black")
hinglish_explanation: Chart ke specific local data point spike area ko focus zoom detailed box dikhane ke liye main plot ke andar dynamic inset axes create karo!
ai_prompt: Show how to construct zoom-in inset axes using indicate_inset_zoom in Matplotlib.

title: Automatic Bar Chart Labels with ax.bar_label()
naive_code:
# Manual loop calculating center offsets to annotate bar heights
for bar in bars:
    yval = bar.get_height()
    ax.text(bar.get_x() + bar.get_width()/2.0, yval, str(yval), ha='center')
wizard_code:
# 1-Liner Native Bar Annotator (Python 3.7+)
bars = ax.bar(categories, heights)
ax.bar_label(bars, padding=3)
hinglish_explanation: Bar chart value annotations offset code calculations complex hote hain. `ax.bar_label(bars)` direct value placement calculations handles karta hai!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate ax.bar_label() with formatting and custom label templates.

title: Clean Grid Lines with Line Style Overrides
naive_code:
# Heavy dark solid grid lines competing with data lines
ax.grid(True)
wizard_code:
# Translucent Dashed Background Grid
ax.grid(True, linestyle='--', alpha=0.5, color='#CBD5E1')
hinglish_explanation: Default grid lines thick and dark hoti hain. Translucent dashed grids (`linestyle='--', alpha=0.5`) chart elements ko pop out hone me help karti hain!
ai_prompt: Show how to display only horizontal or vertical grid lines in Matplotlib.

title: Memory Cleanup with plt.close('all')
naive_code:
# Loop generating charts without closing (causes massive memory leak in memory)
for i in range(100):
    fig, ax = plt.subplots()
    fig.savefig(f'{i}.png')
wizard_code:
# Explicit Figure Memory Release
for i in range(100):
    fig, ax = plt.subplots()
    fig.savefig(f'{i}.png')
    plt.close(fig)  # Release RAM memory reference
hinglish_explanation: Loop me batch charts save karte waqt background widgets hold rehte hain. `plt.close(fig)` immediate cleanup perform karke RAM leak block karta hai!
ai_prompt: Explain memory leakage issues in matplotlib loop generation and how to close figures.

title: Plot Density Scatter with Hexbin Chart via ax.hexbin()

wizard_code:
# Hexagonal Binning Honeycomb Scatter Density Map
hb = ax.hexbin(x, y, gridsize=30, cmap='inferno', mincnt=1)
hinglish_explanation: Heavy overlapping data points scatter scatter plot dense solid ink blobs banata hai. `hexbin` honeycomb ranges counts summarize kar deta hai!
ai_prompt: Show hexbin plot customization with colormap grids and colorbar additions.

title: Annotate Spikes with Arrows via ax.annotate()

wizard_code:

title: Interactive Live Plotting Loops with plt.pause()

wizard_code:

    plt.draw()
